nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=120 beats='Zuri, twelve, is awake past dusk, when the lantern flickers on when their late great-aunt's the brass signal lantern, inherited in spring and silent ever since, clicks itself on from the timer preset nobody ever understood; through the static a calm voice reads five-figure number groups, pauses, and says Moonrise, Moonrise; the voice, recorded long ago, is unmistakably their late great-aunt's; taped under the dial in worn print is a label reading Halcyon LISTENS; Zuri grabs a pencil and copies the groups with her heart drumming'>>

choices.label: Wake her cousin Femi, quietly.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=105 beats='her cousin Femi, sixteen, grumbles awake, hears the voice, and sits bolt upright; he fetches auntie's flag code diary from the shelf while the broadcast repeats on the half hour and promises to end at Moonrise; they wake their uncle, who takes one look at auntie's flag code diary, goes quiet in a remembering way, and sets the rules: stay together, phones charged, bike lights on, her cousin Femi leads, home by breakfast, and text me from wherever this takes you'>>

choices.label: Spread auntie's flag code diary on the kitchen table and decode the numbers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=115 beats='At the kitchen table the diary's flag alphabet gives up its pattern, and the groups resolve one by one into five places they know: the weathered pier, the shuttered picture house, the net shed, the chapel archive, the old windmill, each with its own short hint group; the last line reads Six lanterns shine, then come home to the Lookout; when her cousin Femi swings the swiveling mirror shade, the signal peaks on one steady bearing, east across town, no matter where they stand; the countdown to Moonrise is already running'>>

choices.label: Ride out into their quiet harbor village.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='the market square, five streets spoking away under sodium lamps, the sea a soft breath beyond the rooftops; the brass signal lantern murmurs its patient numbers from her cousin Femi's handlebar basket and the swiveling mirror shade keeps pulling east; keep this scene general so it reads naturally on a first arrival or on any return trip between sites; around them wait the plank walkway up to the weathered pier, the road down to the shuttered picture house, the sea wall, the mossy churchyard to the chapel archive, the gravel track to the old windmill, and always that steady eastward bearing'>>

choices.label: Ride the plank walkway up to the weathered pier.
Coast down Main Street to the shuttered picture house.
Take the sea wall out to the net shed.
Cut across the mossy churchyard to the chapel archive.
Climb the gravel track to the old windmill.
Follow the swiveling mirror shade's steady bearing east to the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the plank walkway climbs between sleeping trees and the weathered pier rises ahead on six riveted legs, the town name painted pale on the tank against the stars; the brass signal lantern murmurs the tower's hint group as they ride; write the approach general enough to read naturally whether this is their first stop of the night or a return'>>

choices.label: Roll up to the tower fence.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The chain-link fence around the weathered pier has a gap by a bent post that every kid in town knows; slipping through, they stand under the tank as if under a giant's table, legs going up forever, a slow drip ticking somewhere in the dark ironwork overhead'>>

choices.label: Cross the gravel to the tower's base.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='In the gravel lot her cousin Femi works the tower's hint group through the diary's flag alphabet and it comes out SIX LEGS, ONE SINGS, READ LOW; around them stand a small maintenance shed with a latch but no lock, the six great legs each stenciled with a number, and up the tank stands the slippery ladder, its rungs already glinting with frost'>>

choices.label: Look inside the pier's bait shed.
Walk the six stenciled legs.
Size up the slippery ladder.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='The slippery ladder is locked, and its rungs glitter with frost; READ LOW nags at Zuri while her cousin Femi zooms his phone camera up the painted letters and finds nothing but paint; either the hint means the answer waits down here, or somebody has to climb around the outside of the slippery ladder in the dark'>>

choices.label: Climb around the outside of the slippery ladder anyway.
Trust READ LOW and go check the legs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='Six legs stenciled 1 to 6 for the maintenance crews, all alike in the torchlight; ONE SINGS; write this beat neutral so it reads the same whether they come from the lot or double back from the slippery ladder; a leg that sings must sound different from its five silent sisters, or else carry some mark the others lack'>>

choices.label: Tap each leg in turn and listen.
Search the legs by torchlight for marks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Knuckles on cold steel: five legs answer with a dead thud, but leg four answers back with a soft creaking groan, something hollow bolted to the steel; at exactly eye height on that leg sits a rusted toolbox'>>

choices.label: Open a rusted toolbox on leg four.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Torchlight slides over rivets and stencils until it snags on leg four: a scratched star at eye height, the same mark that dots the margins of auntie's flag code diary, right beside a rusted toolbox bolted to the steel'>>

choices.label: Open a rusted toolbox under the scratched star.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='a rusted toolbox holds dead fuses and one laminated card in their late great-aunt's print: PAINT KEEPS BETTER THAN PAPER, LITTLE Halcyon; on the card's back is a pencil sketch of a shelf of paint cans; write the find neutral so it reads naturally whichever way the legs led them here, by ringing note or by scratched star'>>

choices.label: Head for the pier's bait shed and its shelves.
Puzzle over a tide-chart scrap with her cousin Femi first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='her cousin Femi turns a tide-chart scrap under his light: in the sketch one can is shaded dark, third from the left; and he works out why paint, of all things: the tank's letters look hand-painted because they were, a volunteer crew repainted this tower once, and a paint store would be exactly where an old painter could hide something'>>

choices.label: Go find that shelf.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the pier's bait shed opens on a latch, no lock: valve charts, a workbench, a shelf of old paint cans, and on the wall a framed photograph of a paint crew up on the catwalk, decades old; keep the scene general so it lands the same whether they walk in cold from the lot or arrive hunting a sketched shelf'>>

choices.label: Go through the paint cans on the shelf.
Take the crew photo down for a closer look.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The caption reads TOWER REPAINT, VOLUNTEERS OF the Halcyon Signal Guild; front row, brush raised, laughing at the camera, stands a young their late great-aunt; on the frame's edge someone chalked a small arrow, long ago, pointing back toward the paint shelf'>>

choices.label: Follow the chalk arrow to the cans.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Among the rusted cans one label is hand-lettered Halcyon BLUE; it lifts far too light for paint, and inside, dry as the day it was hidden, waits a chalk-marked crate; write the moment neutral so it works whether the shelf was searched straight away or found by following the photograph's chalk arrow'>>

choices.label: Unroll the oilcloth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='Inside a chalk-marked crate: a small brass tag shaped like a bird, stamped with the single word Light, and a slip in their late great-aunt's hand remembering the repaint summer, the whole club singing rounds on the catwalk while the paint dried and her keeping time on the rail; beneath it the familiar line, Six lanterns shine, then come home to the Lookout; the tag goes warm in Zuri's pocket and the night is still open'>>

choices.label: Ride back to the market square.
Follow the bearing east to the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Two rungs up the outside of the slippery ladder her trainer skids on frosted steel and her cousin Femi's hand is already on her jacket, easing her down; they sit on the gravel until their hearts slow; the clue said READ LOW, and their late great-aunt never made her club climb anything; palms stinging and pride dented, they ride home under fog rolling off the water, leaving the hunt for another, warmer night; a lesson about frost, taken with dignity'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Main Street is empty and silver; the shuttered picture house's dead marquee still spells half a title in crooked letters; the cinema's hint group works out to ROW J SEAT TWELVE, WHERE THE LIGHT LIVES; the front doors are chained, but a margin note in auntie's flag code diary mentions the alley door in the alley that never latched properly; keep the arrival general for a first visit or a return'>>

choices.label: Slip down the alley to the alley door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the alley door gives with a push, unlatched exactly as auntie's flag code diary promised; inside is dust, old velvet, and the deep held breath of an empty theater; from the dark wings two ways open: the double doors into the auditorium, and a narrow stair marked PROJECTION in flaking gilt'>>

choices.label: Push through into the auditorium.
Take the narrow stair toward the projection booth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The auditorium: rows of seats under dust sheets like a sleeping audience, the screen a pale blank wall; ROW J is out there somewhere under the sheets; and up on the stage, behind the screen's edge, loom the shapes of the great speaker horns the Halcyon Signal Guild once borrowed for its shows'>>

choices.label: Count the rows back to J.
Climb up and cross the stage to the horns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='Row J under its sheet, seats folded shut like all the rest; but seat twelve's armrest carries a small brass plate engraved THE Halcyon'S SEAT, polished brighter than fifty years of dust should allow'>>

choices.label: Kneel and reach under seat twelve.
Fold the seat down and inspect it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Under-seat archaeology: springs, dust, gum gone to stone; then her fingers find a brass armrest plaque's mounting screws from behind, and they are thumb-loose, turned a hundred times, meant to be turned again'>>

choices.label: Turn the loose screws and free the plate.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The seat folds down with a thump that rolls around the empty hall like a single clap; nothing is taped beneath it, but up close a brass armrest plaque sits proud of the wood and rocks under her fingertip, its screws thumb-loose on purpose'>>

choices.label: Unscrew the little plate.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Behind a brass armrest plaque a shallow hollow is carved into the armrest, holding a rolled slip: NOT IN THE DARK, LITTLE Halcyon, THE PRIZE LIVES WHERE THE LIGHT LIVES; every eye in this hall spent its evenings looking away from the answer, up at the little window where the light comes from; write the find neutral for either way of reaching it, from under the seat or over it'>>

choices.label: Head for the projection booth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='Up the side steps onto the stage; behind the screen's edge the horn alcove waits, but the warped floorboard on this side flexes under her toe with a groan, dark pit space showing through the gaps'>>

choices.label: Edge along the solid boards by the wall.
Hurry straight across before you lose your nerve.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The speaker horns rise taller than her cousin Femi, mouths full of dark, the machines that once gave the Halcyon Signal Guild its thunder; chalked neatly on the biggest horn's frame are a star and the words EVERY SHOW ENDS AT THE BOOTH'>>

choices.label: Find the stair up to the projection booth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='The narrow stair coils up past a crooked PRIVATE sign to the booth landing, where the door stands ajar on darkness and a dusty peephole looks in beside it; write this landing neutral so it reads naturally from any direction the theater has funneled them'>>

choices.label: Push the booth door open.
Look through a dusty peephole first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Through the glass: the projector crouched like a sleeping brass animal, a splicing bench, and a wall rack of film tins in neat rows, except for a mismatched film can standing nose-out of line as if it has been waiting to be noticed'>>

choices.label: Go in to a mismatched film can.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='a mismatched film can in the booth smells of oil and dust gone cold decades ago; the rack's labels are hand-inked, and it reads The Harbor Serenade, FINAL REEL, in their late great-aunt's print; the projector's lamp housing stands open and threaded, ready, as if someone always meant for one more show; keep the room neutral for a first or return visit'>>

choices.label: Open a mismatched film can.
Thread the reel and roll the show.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='No film inside; a mismatched film can is lined with felt and holds a small brass tag stamped Finds, and a slip in their late great-aunt's hand: The Harbor Serenade, once a year the Halcyon Signal Guild filled this hall with a radio play through the big horns, and she voiced the storm so well the front row put their umbrellas up; beneath, the line, Six lanterns shine, then come home to the Lookout; the tag clinks into Zuri's pocket'>>

choices.label: Back to the market square.
Follow the bearing east to the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='The warped floorboard cracks and her leg drops knee-deep into the under-stage dark; her cousin Femi has her by both arms before the fright even lands; a scraped shin, a torn sock, a long careful crawl back to the solid wings; on the alley steps they take stock and call it honestly: no treasure is worth a broken ankle in an empty building at night; they wedge the alley door shut behind them and pedal home limping-proud, the hunt paused, not lost'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='The projector clatters awake and the screen fills, not with film stars but with the club's own home footage: this very hall packed for The Harbor Serenade, paper tickets waving, and through some past hand's patching of projector to horns rolls their late great-aunt's storm scene, wind and thunder made of voice and tray-rattled tin; Zuri and her cousin Femi sink into row J and watch until the reel runs out and the booth window goes gray; the hunt can wait a night, they have seen the show'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the sea wall: masts ticking, halyards ringing their small bells, black water breathing slow against the quay; out along the far quay stands the net shed, and past it one window of the harbor office glows yellow, somebody awake at this hour; write the approach general for a first ride out or a return'>>

choices.label: Ride out along the sea wall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='the sea wall's lamps space out pools of light with long dark gaps between; far out in the channel a bell buoy clanks its slow patient note, the sound this town falls asleep to; ahead wait the net shed's big rolling doors and, beyond them, the lit office window'>>

choices.label: Come up to the net shed's doors.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the net shed's rolling doors wear a serious padlock; the office window's light spills yellow onto the quay stones a little farther on; and below the shed's seaward side, leaky rowboat is tied at the water steps near a small hatch above the tideline, though it sits oddly low in the water, paint peeling and oars dusty, the look of a boat nobody has trusted in a long while'>>

choices.label: Knock at the lit harbor office.
Check the shed doors up close.
Row the leaky rowboat around to the sea hatch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='The padlock is solid and the hasp is welded on, but the old doors have shrunk with age and a torch-wide gap runs between them; and among the faded harbor stencils on the planks one small painted shape is no stencil at all: a star'>>

choices.label: Shine the torch through the gap.
Go knock at the office for the key.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Through the gap the torch finds a herd of huge buoys sleeping on their cradles, cones and cans and cages; and at the very back an old bell buoy, its bell still hung, something in its shadowed cage just catching the thin beam of light'>>

choices.label: Only one way in: knock at the office.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='The harbor office: kettle, charts, a VHF murmuring the shipping weather, and Mr. Okafor the harbormaster keeping the pre-dawn watch; on his desk lies a pad of penciled five-figure groups in a neat old-fashioned hand, tonight's the patient dusk flashing, copied; write his greeting warm and general so it lands the same on a first knock of the night or on a return visit'>>

choices.label: Show him your decoded page.
Ask about the numbers on his pad.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='He reads Zuri's page and goes very still; that is Halcyon's grid, he says; he was the Halcyon Signal Guild's youngest member and copied his first net at this very desk; he cannot leave the watch until the day man comes, but he slides a sea-salted brass key across the counter: the bell buoy at the back, and their late great-aunt would want a Adeyemi to be the one to open it'>>

choices.label: Take a sea-salted brass key.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='The pad's top sheet is tonight; underneath lie older sheets in the same hand but rounder and younger, nets he copied here as a teenager and never threw away; he taps the small star doodled in the margin: the Halcyon Signal Guild, the midnight club of a night-shift town, cannery and harbor and hospital, and their late great-aunt the best of them; then he reaches into the drawer for a sea-salted brass key'>>

choices.label: Take a sea-salted brass key he holds out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='a sea-salted brass key is warm from his hand; the kettle has just boiled and the cocoa tin is already down off the shelf; outside, the net shed waits in the dark and the brass signal lantern keeps murmuring its countdown; write the moment neutral for either doorway that brought them to it'>>

choices.label: Go open the net shed now.
Stay for a mug and his Halcyon stories.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='a sea-salted brass key turns a small side door and the buoy herd looms up in torchlight, iron and salt and tar; at the back on its cradle rests the retired bell buoy, bell still hung in its cage, and the hint group said THE RETIRED BELL REMEMBERS'>>

choices.label: Ring the old bell, softly.
Search the buoy's cage frame by torchlight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='One soft knuckle-ring and the note fills the whole shed, round and low, the other buoys humming along; and inside the ringing she hears a rattle that does not belong: under the bell's skirt, the clapper chain carries a small brass cylinder swaged into its links'>>

choices.label: Unscrew the little cylinder.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Fingers along the cage frame find a patch of newer paint; under the torch it resolves into a star and a painted arrow pointing up into the bell's dark throat, where her reaching knuckles knock the clapper and something on its chain rattles in answer'>>

choices.label: Work the little cylinder off the chain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='The cylinder unscrews: a small brass tag stamped Every, and a rolled slip in their late great-aunt's hand about the fog nights, the club taking turns on the sea wall ringing answers out to the bell buoy so nobody at sea felt alone, because a voice finds you in the fog, little Halcyon; and beneath it the line, Six lanterns shine, then come home to the Lookout; write the find neutral for either path through the bell'>>

choices.label: Ride back to the market square.
Follow the bearing east to the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Two oar-pulls out and the leaky rowboat is ankle-deep, hauled out for caulking and never meant to float tonight; they put back in an undignified hurry, boots full of harbor, and stand dripping on the steps while the bell buoy clanks somewhere out in the dark, unbothered; her cousin Femi quotes the rule from auntie's flag code diary's margin, never go on the water tired; soaked socks, warm shame, and a slow ride home with the hunt saved for a drier night'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=100 beats='The cocoa goes down and the stories come out: the night the Halcyon Signal Guild counted a trawler home through fog by bell-rings, the Christmas net with paper hats over headphones, the way their late great-aunt could tune a signal out of pure noise like coaxing a cat off a roof; the day man arrives with the gulls and the sky already pearl-gray, Moonrise spent, the net shed unopened; but the club is alive in the telling, and Mr. Okafor shakes both their hands like colleagues; they will be back, and he will be listening'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Cutting across the mossy churchyard toward the dark library and its one lit porch lamp; the chapel archive's hint group works out to MAP ROOM, THE DRAWER OF THE FOUNDING YEAR, MIND THE BELL; quick in, quick out, her cousin Femi says, this one is a pit stop; keep the approach general for any point in the night'>>

choices.label: Roll up to the side porch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='The service door under the porch lamp; above the lintel the library's stone owl stares down, and above the door frame hangs a small black box holding the night chime, marked MIND THE BELL; everyone's gran knows the librarians have hidden an after-hours key up behind the owl since before their uncle was born'>>

choices.label: Feel up behind the stone owl for the key.
Just try the handle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Her fingers find the worn brass key on its ancient nail behind the owl's wing; the lock turns as if oiled for exactly this, the door opens on the smell of paper and floor wax, and the chime above the frame keeps its peace'>>

choices.label: Slip inside.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='The reading hall by moonlight, tables silver, stacks fading into the dark; the chapel archive's glass door waits at the far end, and midway one green-shaded lamp has been left burning over a display table, a small island of light in the hush'>>

choices.label: Go straight for the chapel archive.
Detour past the lamp-lit display table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The display is half-built, town anniversaries pinned on card; one card reads the Halcyon Signal Guild, FOUNDED 1975, beside a photograph of a dozen night-shift faces crowded around a kitchen table of radios; the founding year, handed to them in passing, like the library meant to help'>>

choices.label: On to the chapel archive.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the chapel archive is one wide cabinet of flat drawers labeled by year, the town on paper a hundred times over; THE DRAWER OF THE FOUNDING YEAR; and on one drawer's label, visible only up close, someone long ago inked a tiny star; write the room neutral for either way in, direct or by way of the display table'>>

choices.label: Open the drawer with the little inked star.
Work along the drawers year by year.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Drawer after drawer whispers open, coastlines and street grids sliding past under the torch; then the beam snags on a label inked with a tiny star beside its year, 1975, and the hunt through the years is over'>>

choices.label: Slide that drawer wide.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='the christening-year chest: the whole town in fine ink, and drawn onto it five faded circles, the weathered pier, the shuttered picture house, the net shed, this very library, the old windmill, with pencil lines running from all five to one spot on the old rail spur east of the fairgrounds, marked with a tiny drawn bird; taped in the drawer's corner, a capsule holding a small brass tag stamped Wandering and a slip in their late great-aunt's hand, the night we founded the club we divided the town into listening posts, and I circled my favorites; the map says on paper what the swiveling mirror shade has hummed all night, everything points east; write the find neutral for either route to this drawer'>>

choices.label: Ease the drawer shut and head back to the market square.
Follow the map's pencil lines east to the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='The handle turns a quarter-inch and the chime goes off like a tin thunderstorm over their heads; a light comes on in the librarian's cottage across the green; they stand and wait, honest, while Ms. Kettle arrives in dressing gown and slippers, silences the chime with one practiced whack, and hears them out; she is kinder than her chime but firm: the chapel archive by daylight, with her, and their uncle hears about tonight from you and no one else; a red-eared walk home'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='the gravel track up the old windmill starts by the allotment gates; high above, the mast's red beacon pulses slow as a heartbeat against the stars; two ways climb from here, the long patient switchbacks or the crumbling stone stair the big kids brag about; keep the foot of the hill general for any hour of the night'>>

choices.label: Take the long switchbacks.
Scramble straight up the crumbling stone stair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The switchbacks zigzag up through gorse; the town drops away below them, a circuit board of sleeping streetlamps edged by the dark sea; breath, pedal-push, walk the steep bits, and always the beacon's slow red pulse drawing nearer overhead'>>

choices.label: Climb into the wind at the shoulder of the hill.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='At the hill's shoulder the night wind takes over, and the wind up here is singing: a low creaking hum, hanging in the air, swelling and fading, the mast's guy wires played like harp strings; the hint group said WHERE THE WIRES SING, THE THIRD ANCHOR'>>

choices.label: Follow the singing up to the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The mast compound at the top: a locked gate, a DANGER KEEP OUT sign, the great mast humming to itself inside its fence; but the guy wires leave the fence high overhead and slope down into the open bracken, down to concrete anchor blocks that no fence guards at all; THE THIRD ANCHOR is out there in the dark'>>

choices.label: Trace the wires out to their anchor blocks.
Stand a moment and listen to the hill sing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Ear tipped to the wind: each wire holds its own note, and the chord slowly turns as gusts walk across the hill, the whole summit one instrument; their late great-aunt stood here, both of them know it without saying so; the wires slope away downhill like strings on a tilted harp, pointing to their anchors'>>

choices.label: Follow the wires down to the anchors.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=95 beats='The anchor blocks sit out in the bracken, concrete shoulders with steel plates stamped A1, A2, A3; the third anchor stands a little apart, beside a weathered millstone; write the blocks neutral so the scene reads the same whether they came straight down or stayed to listen first'>>

choices.label: Examine anchor three's steel plate.
Check a weathered millstone beside it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='A3's plate carries a ring of bolts, all weathered gray except one: an iron crank handle stamped with a tiny star, and it turns under her fingers, threaded smooth, as if someone has greased it faithfully once a year'>>

choices.label: Turn an iron crank handle out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='a weathered millstone's brass benchmark cap is engraved with the mapmakers' arrow, and around the arrow someone has added a ring of tiny stamped stars and one scratched line pointing straight at the base of anchor three, where an iron crank handle glints brass among the gray'>>

choices.label: Go turn an iron crank handle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='an iron crank handle draws a small cover plate with it, and behind the plate is a dry cavity cast into the concrete itself, made when the anchor was poured; whoever built this cache built it to outlast everything on the hill; inside waits a brass cylinder; write the find neutral for either clue that led here, plate or pillar'>>

choices.label: Unscrew the cylinder.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='The tag inside reads Soul; the slip remembers the night the Halcyon Signal Guild strung a longwire from this mast and heard Japan, Alaska, a ship off Chile, our little town talking to the whole turning Earth, and from this hill, little Halcyon, everything is in range; beneath it, the line, Six lanterns shine, then come home to the Lookout'>>

choices.label: Stand up and look out from the hill.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The whole town lies below, harbor to fairgrounds, lamps going out one by one; her cousin Femi swings the brass signal lantern's loop slowly and the numbers peak, not at the mast at all but down the far slope, east past the fairgrounds where the old rail spur runs, and there among the dark fields one small amber window is lit'>>

choices.label: Coast back down to the market square.
Ride the far slope straight for the Lookout.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='Halfway up, the crumbling stone stair moves; not a fall, a slither, the whole slope of loose stone walking out from under them until they are back at the bottom sitting down hard, palms skinned, one bike wheel still spinning; they patch up with the tape in her cousin Femi's bag and take honest stock: the hill is not going anywhere, but skin only grows back so fast; they walk the bikes home under fog rolling off the water, and the switchbacks will still be there tomorrow'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Following the swiveling mirror shade's steady bearing east, past the fairgrounds fence and onto the grass-grown old rail spur; write this arrival fully neutral so it reads naturally whenever in the night it happens and whatever has come before it; the numbers grow in the brass signal lantern and then, faintly, arrive from somewhere ahead too, the same voice a half-beat early, the night speaking in stereo with itself; down the rails stands a squat brick-and-wood signal box with one amber-lit upper window, a longwire running from its roof to a tall ash tree'>>

choices.label: Walk the rails to the lit signal box.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='The signal box up close: brick base, wooden upper storey, outside stairs to a landing door; a weathered board reads MILEPOST 7 SIGNAL BOX, and beneath it a smaller painted sign, the Lookout, the Halcyon Signal Guild, EST. 1975; the door holds an old lever lock, the sash window glows amber above the stair rail, and through the glass the patient voice reads on; keep this entry visit-neutral for any point in the night'>>

choices.label: Hunt for the key a Halcyon would leave.
Stretch from the stair rail to the rusted-shut hatch.
Put an ear to the landing door first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='Through the door: the voice, the soft clunk of relays, the hiss of tape moving reel to reel, machinery keeping a decades-long appointment all by itself; and behind the numbers, faint as a pulse, a clock ticking toward Moonrise'>>

choices.label: Go find the key.
Sit on the stairs and witness the last broadcast from out here.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='Where would radio people leave a key; the landing door has a lintel under fifty years of paint, and over by the antenna anchor on the ash tree hangs a carved driftwood box, sitting exactly where a longwire needs tending; write the hunt neutral for either doorway that brought them to it'>>

choices.label: Open a carved driftwood box on the ash tree.
Feel along the door lintel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='Nothing on the lintel but paint layers and one spider with opinions; but from up on the stair rail, eye level with the tree, a carved driftwood box by the antenna anchor looks freshly weatherproofed for something supposedly fifty years old'>>

choices.label: Go open a carved driftwood box.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='a carved driftwood box's floor slides out like a matchbox drawer; inside, wrapped in wax paper, lies a key, and with it a small tag in their late great-aunt's print: FOR WHOEVER IS STILL LISTENING; write the find neutral for either path that led to the tree'>>

choices.label: Climb the stairs and unlock the door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The key turns a lock kept oiled by its builder's long care; the landing door swings onto warm amber light, and the voice is suddenly clear and close, patient, reading its groups to the room the way it has all night'>>

choices.label: Step inside.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='From the doorway: a signal box operating floor turned radio shack, the old lever frame gleaming down one wall, and on the instrument shelf the station itself, tape reels turning, valves glowing amber, a clockwork timer ticking beside them; the floor is swept and the dust sheets are folded; this is a room that has been waiting, not abandoned'>>

choices.label: Come all the way in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='The room holds three pulls at once: a wall of photographs and postcards behind the desk; auntie's flag code diary lying open on the desk beside a microphone on its stand; and the humming rack itself, reels and valves and the timer's brass wheel creeping toward its last notch, with a card in a stand reading FINAL TRANSMISSION ENDS AT Moonrise; keep the room's welcome neutral, it reads the same whenever the night delivers its visitors'>>

choices.label: Study the wall of photographs.
Read auntie's flag code diary, open on the desk.
Go straight to the humming station.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='The photographs tell the whole story plainly, whatever the night has already shown: the Halcyon Signal Guild, night-shift faces in headphones and paper hats, their late great-aunt young at this very desk; captions in her print; and one typed card at the end: when the cannery closed and the club scattered, their late great-aunt built this station and her hunt so the town would keep one more net in it; recap only these path-universal facts, the patient dusk flashing, the bearing, the countdown, what this wall itself shows; the sky in the window is turning'>>

choices.label: Stay at the wall, reading every caption as the sky pales.
Turn to the station while there is still time.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='auntie's flag code diary's last entry is in their late great-aunt's hand, dated the spring she died: TO WHOEVER FINDS the Lookout, at Moonrise today the station ends its watch; you may let her sign off as built, keep her singing, or give her to the town, and any of the three is right; sign the log, little Halcyon, whoever you are; below runs a page of members' callsigns and telephone numbers, most gently crossed through, a few not'>>

choices.label: Carry auntie's flag code diary over to the humming rack.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='At the rack the timer's brass wheel sits close to its last cam; the microphone waits on the desk, wired through a toggle labeled in their late great-aunt's print, LISTEN and SPEAK; the voice reads on, pausing between groups as if leaving room for someone; keep this decision beat visit-neutral and recap only path-universal facts, the patient dusk flashing, the eastward bearing, the countdown now nearly spent; however much of her trail the night has held, it ends in this room, and what becomes of the Lookout is theirs to choose before Moonrise'>>

choices.label: Flip the toggle to SPEAK and answer her.
Find out how to keep the station alive past today.
Give the Lookout to the people it was built from.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The toggle clicks to SPEAK; the reels keep turning, recording now as well as playing, because their late great-aunt built her station to listen back; the next pause between groups opens like a held breath; and on the desk stands a small frame holding a typed sign-off script laid out in her careful style'>>

choices.label: Answer in your own words.
Read her framed sign-off script.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Zuri keys the mic and says it plainly: her name, whose granddaughter she is, and that the town is still listening; her voice goes onto the same tape as their late great-aunt's, the two of them together in the archive now; the toggle rests under her hand, the reels turn, and fog rolling off the water is fast beyond the window'>>

choices.label: Flip back to LISTEN and wait in the hiss.
Sign the logbook and let the station finish its watch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The framed script is the club's old net-closing, ending with the lines THIS IS Halcyon, THE NET IS CLOSED, Light Finds Every Wandering Soul, 73 AND GOODNIGHT; read aloud in this room, the closing line lands like the whole night said out loud at last; the timer's wheel edges toward its final cam while she steadies her breath'>>

choices.label: Read it exactly as written and let her close.
Read it, then add one promise before the cam trips.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The shelf holds a maintenance folder in their late great-aunt's print, diagrams of the timer, the tape path, the winding crank, all kept simple on purpose, teachable; so the station can keep singing, the only real question is who its keepers will be and how far they will take the keeping'>>

choices.label: Wind the timer for one more year, same midnight.
Plan to learn the station properly, licenses and all.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Together they follow the diagram, crank the clockwork until the spring sits firm, and re-cue the reels; the station takes the winding like an old cat settling deeper into its chair; next year, same frequency, same midnight; and the key goes back into a carved driftwood box afterward, because keepers put things back where Halcyons can find them'>>

choices.label: Shake on it in the amber light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='By torch and desk lamp they copy what matters into her cousin Femi's phone: the frequency, the timer diagram, the manual's title, and the few uncrossed names on auntie's flag code diary's last page who could still teach them; the plan builds itself on the ride home, licenses, callsigns, lessons; the station will not just survive, it will be understood'>>

choices.label: Start the long apprenticeship at Moonrise.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the Lookout was built out of people, night-shifters and neighbors and family; auntie's flag code diary's page of telephone numbers sits open on the desk and her cousin Femi's phone sits charged in his pocket, and between them run two roads: bring family here now, before the watch ends, or use the station's own voice to hand the hunt onward'>>

choices.label: Call their uncle to come, right now, before Moonrise.
Record a brand-new hunt onto her tape for the town's kids.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='their uncle answers on the first ring, awake after all, and the headlights come bumping up the rail lane in minutes; she climbs into the amber light, sees their late great-aunt's handwriting on every label and lever, and puts her hand over her mouth; the three of them fit in front of the desk exactly, as if the room had been measured for it'>>

choices.label: Stand together for the last groups.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='With minutes left they key the mic and read five new groups of their own, planned fast in fierce whispers, five new places worth finding, and a recorded promise that the Lookout will call again; the machine takes their voices in and keeps them beside their late great-aunt's, the answer reel winding the future on next to the past'>>

choices.label: Sign the log as the new hunt-makers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=90 beats='The stretch from the stair rail is longer than it looked and the rusted-shut hatch is painted shut anyway; a boot slips, a shin barks on the rail, and her cousin Femi hauls her back by the jacket with both their hearts going like drum solos; sitting on the stairs they agree on the obvious, nobody breaks into the Lookout, least of all family; the amber window glows on above them as they head home to come back with better ideas, and daylight'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='One more caption, then one more, and then the voice changes: the numbers stop and the sign-off begins while they are still at the wall; by the time they reach the desk the words are already ending and the reels wind down with a long sigh; Moonrise, exactly as promised; they missed the moment to answer, but they stood in the right room and heard her say goodnight; they lock up softly, slide the key back into a carved driftwood box for the next listener, and walk home planning the telling of it'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='They sit shoulder to shoulder on the wooden stairs and let the last broadcast play out through the door: groups, pauses, and at Moonrise a sign-off in that beloved voice, heard through weathered wood with the first birds starting up around them; some doors you open, and some watches you simply witness; they walk home full and quiet, saving the question of the key for daylight and their uncle, while the amber light clicks off behind them like a goodnight'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=105 beats='The hiss holds, and then, one by one, voices: an old careful voice from across town that might be keeping a harbor watch, then farther ones, callsigns surfacing out of the static like lights coming on, old Halcyons who never quite stopped listening and heard the final night go out; Zuri takes the check-ins the way her grandmother must have, name by name, while Moonrise fills the window; the net, it turns out, was never closed, it was only waiting for a voice at this desk'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='She signs the logbook beneath their late great-aunt's final entry, granddaughter under grandmother; at Moonrise the voice reads its last group, says its goodnight, and the reels stop with her answer safely inside the archive, the last voice on the tape a Adeyemi once again; the station finishes its watch complete, and Zuri walks out into the birdsong carrying the key and the whole story home to breakfast'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='Zuri reads it exactly as written, steady as she can manage: THIS IS Halcyon, THE NET IS CLOSED, Light Finds Every Wandering Soul, 73 AND GOODNIGHT; the cam trips, the reels slow, the valve glow fades like embers banked for the night; the station ends its watch precisely as its builder designed, closed in family voice; they sign the log, return the key to a carved driftwood box, and walk home through Moonrise letting the quiet be part of the gift'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='She reads the closing as written, and then, before the cam can trip, adds one line of her own: the Lookout will open again, same night next year; to make the promise true they wind the timer together until the spring sits firm; the station accepts its encore, one more year, wound and waiting; walking home through the gray-gold light they are already keeping a secret the size of a year, and it fits in their pockets like a brass key'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='Hands are shaken in the amber light, and the Lantern Line has keepers again; through the year that follows they come back on quiet nights to dust and wind and listen, learning the room by heart; and when the station next wakes the town, two more voices are on the tape welcoming whoever is clever enough to find it; the hunt stands ready, the Lookout stays warm, the watch goes on'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='Months of Saturdays later there are two framed licenses on the Lookout's wall beneath their late great-aunt's photographs, two new callsigns inked into the logbook, and the last uncrossed names of the old club teaching them code over cocoa; when the station next speaks it will be maintained, understood, and loved by keepers who know every valve by its first name; the watch continues, properly crewed'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='Three of them stand shoulder to shoulder as the station reads its last groups; their uncle's hand finds Zuri's on one side and her cousin Femi's on the other; the sign-off comes in the voice all three of them know best, and nobody needs to say anything at all; when the reels stop, their uncle signs the logbook beneath her children's names, and they leave together into Moonrise, a family handed its inheritance while it could still be heard'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='The next time the Lantern Line wakes, a year on, other kids' windows light up around town; five new sites, built through a summer of family workdays and borrowed ladders; their late great-aunt's voice opens the broadcast and Zuri's closes it, a living voice at each end of the night; the hunt is no longer a memorial, it is a tradition, passed on exactly the way Halcyons would have wanted'>>
